title: Virginia Commonwealth University - 5 Things I Wish I Knew Before Attending
body: I don't understand why anyone really needs an adviser. I spent 4 years at VCU....I read the catalog, read the requirements of my major, and understood the number of credits and courses I needed in order to graduate. It's your education, so take some initiative and learn what is required of you as a students and what is needed to graduate. Read the catalog, it's all there.
author: @dbreardon

title: Virginia Commonwealth University - 5 Things I Wish I Knew Before Attending
body: You can’t take classes outside your major. VCU Art students are really the only ones who can take art classes. Wish I knew that.
author: @ArinGokdemir

title: Virginia Commonwealth University - 5 Things I Wish I Knew Before Attending
body: VCU didn’t require a bike, nor did that dining hall exist.  Academic Advisers are Graduates that know more.  Don’t hate there.

title: Virginia Commonwealth University - 5 Things I Wish I Knew Before Attending
body: I have been to the VCU dining hall many times, all days of the week and times of day. I have never seen any difference in the quality of the food. It is all good stuff. I am not sure where this point is coming from as it has not been my experience at all. Maybe something has changed since this person attended.
author: @FoScoJo

title: Virginia Commonwealth University - 5 Things I Wish I Knew Before Attending
body: Most schools are attempting to accommodate other races -- some are traditionally white and trying to accommodate blacks.  Others are traditionally black and trying to accommodate whites.  VCU has always been a well-integrated school.  When I was there in the early 1980s, I lived and ate with black students in the medical school cafeteria, while the current Virginia governor was attending a different medical school and dressing in blackface for the yearbook.
author: @crimony3054

title: Virginia Commonwealth University - 5 Things I Wish I Knew Before Attending
body: VCU does not ensure the safety of its students. Reason enough not to go. See video of students getting assaulted at the Students for Life meeting.
author: @24601cc

title: VCU Campus Tour
body: It's almost like another world now......since I'm a grad from 40 years ago!!!! So much has changed and the campus has grown so. There are still some familiar icons, like the Mosque, Monroe Park and the Cathedral and the OLDDDD dorms (I can't believe Johnson and Rhoads are still there!!!). But all the new facilities, especially along Broad St. are astounding. The only thing I would lament is the students now have no concept of being in Richmond when it still was "a city"...with a bustling downtown. I used to love the energy plus got to work part-time at Miller & Rhoades Dept. store to help with my expenses. We didn't feel so confined to the campus, per se. I do wish I'd had many of the tools and resources the school now offers, however!!
author: @D.C.22

title: Asking UVA Students: Which Colleges Did YOU Reject To Come To UVA?
body:
My son had an excellent education at state universities: bachelors degree at the University of Maryland, College Park; masters degree at University of California at Santa Barbara; and second masters degree at Penn State University. He received scholarships for these two masters degrees.
As for me, I attended two state schools: bachelors degree at University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill; and master of social work at University of Maryland, Baltimore.
author: @maralynfarber2068

title: Asking UVA Students: Which Colleges Did YOU Reject To Come To UVA?
body: I rejected UVa to go to Va Tech because it's just so much stronger in Engineering. Anyone who chose UVA over VT for engineering is a blue-blood schlockmonger. Va Tech will get you much more respect in the engineering world.

title: VCU Rhoads Dorm Tour!
body: What MEMORIES your video brings back!  (YOU are the spitting image of a girl on my floor from ROANOKE, VA.!!!!).  I lived in RHODES HALL (ALL GIRLS!) for 2 yrs. ,  on the 9th floor (FACING JOHNSON HALL) IN 1971!  I MOVED DOWN TO 9, BECAUSE I WAS ON 18 & FELT THE BUILDING 'SWAY' - INTOLERABLE!!!!   Can't believe it's STILL STANDING!!!!  I HAD THE TIME OF MY LIFE THERE, 6 HOURS FROM HOME (& 'OUT OF STATERS' WEREN'T ALLOWED TO HAVE A  CAR!!!!  LOST 60 LBS. BY DEC. !!!!).  WE'RE ITALIAN, SO TO ME THE COOKED FOOD WAS AWFUL, SO I LIVED ON JELLO & SALAD!  I TOO HAD A ROOMMATE WHO WENT BACK HOME THE FIRST WEEK (HOMESICK, & ONLY from PETERSBURG, VA. 45 MINS. AWAY, LOL!).  I GOT A NEW ROOMIE IN AN HOUR & WE BECAME CLOSE FRIENDS!  MY JUNIOR YEAR I TRANSFERRED TO THE UINV. OF MD. TO CHANGE MAJORS & GET A BEAUTIFUL CAMPUS!   GREAT MOVE.....THANKS FOR THE 'TOUR' DOWN MEMORY LANE!  (TENSION RODS & CURTAINS, MAKE A  GREAT DIFFERENCE & COZY UP THE STERILE ROOM....they ALSO MAKE STICK ON WALLPAPER & BORDERS NOW, TOO !!).   I WAS GOING TO BE AN ART TEACHER, BUT BECAME A PROFESSIONAL INTERIOR DESIGNER INSTEAD!  GOOD LUCK TO YOU IN ALL YOUR ENDEAVORS!!!!!!!
author: @karenhayman3848

title: #college tour of #uva #universityofvirginia #backtoschool #fall2023 #collegelife #vlog #students
body:
He centered Grounds around the Rotunda (library) instead of the uva chapel because he believed knowledge—not religion—should be at the core of a university and that education is the foundation of a democracy.

yes he believed in the separation of church and state, but the Rotunda is symbol of holding the pursuit of knowledge life's greatest endeavor, not the worship of gods.
author: @RobertMJohnson
